mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, providing significantly higher throughput and lower resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring high-performance caching and data storage. Key trade-offs include compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached APIs versus the need for migration to a new system.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install Dragonfly easily.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for Dragonfly.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dragonfly to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dragonfly application into your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the desired namespace and version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to define the configuration for your Dragonfly instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Dragonfly instance with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dragonfly pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Lists all pods in the 'dragonfly' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dragonfly from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all Dragonfly resources and any associated data may be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly release.
> Deletes the Dragonfly namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade Dragonfly to a newer version, including backing up data and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Backs up the current Dragonfly configuration.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Dragonfly pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get dragonflies -n dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Dragonfly" in version "dragonflydb.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly CRDs may not be installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dragonfly application may be misconfigured or lacking resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dragonfly and adjust resource requests/limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may be under heavy load or the Dragonfly service is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster resource usage with: kubectl top nodes and ensure that the Dragonfly service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Dragonfly service is not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly or the pods are not ready.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with: kubectl get svc -n dragonfly and check pod readiness with: kubectl get pods -n dragonfly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Redis commands and improved memory efficiency.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing: kubectl get pods -n dragonfly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
